rulings.Case Title: LARRY PAMBID VS. GUADALUPE GAMINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion Tpo Dismiss Per Code Of Civil Procedure Section 583.310
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT GUADALUPE GAMINO'S Motion To Dismiss Per Code Of Civil Procedure Section 583.310. Granted. Five years from the date Plaintiff's Complaint was filed elapsed on February 22, 2012 once the necessary time periods are excluded from computation under section 583.340(c). Due to impossibility, impracticability, or futility, this action is tolled during the time when the default and default judgment were in effect (October 31, 2007 through July 31, 2008) and the time when the dismissal was in effect (August 31, 2011 thorugh November 14, 2011). Plaintiff has failed to show that any further tolling is warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ATHOS EQUIPMENT D'ESCRIME, LLC VS. ALEXEY KUZNETSOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plt'S Request For Inspection And Production Of Documents (Set One) To Defts For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ATHOS EQUIPMENT D'ESCRIME, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Inspection And Production Of Documents (Set One) and For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel GRANTED. Order to Issue at hearing on the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MCNAIR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 30, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and ANN KIM MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Plaintiff cannot establish his breach of contract cause of action because plaintiff fails to establish the existence of a written contract that complies with section 6.102(6) of the San Francisco Charter. The intentional tort cause of action fails based on the litigation privilege. (Wang v. Heck (2012) 203 Cal. App, 4th 677; Shaddox v. Bertani (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1406). HIPAA does not preempt the litigation privilege. The purpose of HIPAA is to protect medical confidences, not provide a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YUE YING WANG VS. NATURAL WONDER A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANTS YUE WANG, MAN GUAN, WILLIE YEE, JERRY LO and MG BEAUTY CARE's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled in its entirety. The first, second, fourth and fifth claims are based at least in part on factual allegations that are different from the allegations that are the basis of the third claim for misappropriation of trade secrets. While conspiracy is technically not a stand alone claim, it is a basis to impute liability and thus it is appropriately alleged. Nothing other than additional cost to the parties and delay would be achieved by sustaining the demurrer as to the sixth cause of action with leave to amend to allege the conspiracy allegations in the introductory facts, so the court declines to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HERMAN HU D.D.S., A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Plaintiff owes the taxes per section 902.1(a). Because the language of the ordinance is clear and unequivocal, statements made by city officials contrary to the language of section 902.1(a) are not entitled to any weight and do not support an estoppel argument. The estoppel argument also fails because the plaintiff does not present any evidence to support such an argument. The tax, as applied to the plaintiff, is not an impermissible income tax. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 22, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL BUTLER'S Motion To File Third Amended Complaint is Denied. Plaintiff failed to provide a proposed Third Amended Complaint as required by California Rule of Court 3.1324(a)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GRANT INTL CO. INC., A CORPORATION VS. THE WATT NETWORK LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF GRANT INTL CO. INC., A CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion For Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED in part. Order to Issue at hearing on the motion. Moving party to bring copy of proposed Order to hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COBLENTZ, PATCH, DUFFY & BASS, LLP, VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 24, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action. Plaintiff's objection to defendants' request for judicial notice of plaintiff's tax refund claim is sustained. All other requests for judicial notice are granted. The first cause of action is deficient because the City's payroll tax as applied to "equity" partners can, by its terms, covers "compensation for services" received by such partners, whether or not such compensation is paid in the form of "guaranteed payments." Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege, if it wishes to do so and can do so in good faith, that it is entitled to a refund because the amount of the payments made to its equity partners as compensation for services was less than the amount of payments made to those partners that formed the basis for the tax assessed and collected. The complaint and the judicially noticeable materials do not show that Proposition Q violated Proposition 218 or section 17041.5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE SCOTT VS. HEALTH NET OF CALIFORNIA, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 29, 2012, Line 31, DEFENDANT HILL PHYSICIANS MEDICAL GROUP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. The fourth and fifth claims are not uncertain because defendant can reasonably respond to them. Plaintiff adequately pleads a cause of action for unfair and fraudulent business practices. Plaintiff pleads injury in fact and may be able to obtain injunctive relief against this defendant. The negligence cause of action is also adequately pled. Plaintiffs pleads that defendant's denial of her medically necessary treatment has caused harm to herAny party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.
